sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the BR corner (37, 18) of the magenta square at (36, 17) didn't move it. The square is still at (36, 17). So clicking on the BR corner doesn't move the square.
Let me try clicking on the BL corner (37, 17) to see if it moves left.
Wait, but I already know that clicking on the BL corner moves the square left by 1. Let me verify this.
Actually, looking at my earlier findings:
- Click TL corner → move up-left by 1
- Click TR corner → move up by 1
- Click BL corner → move left by 1
- Click BR corner → no movement (or maybe it does something else)
But wait, the square at (36, 17) is to the left of the blue circle (which is at cols 29-37). If I can only move up and left, I can't move this square into the circle.
Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe the goal is not to move all magenta squares into the circle. Maybe the goal is to have a certain number of magenta squares inside the circle, or to match the colors in the top bar.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The top bar has 4 colored squares: pink, magenta, purple, yellow. Maybe the goal is to have one magenta square inside the blue circle, and the other magenta squares outside. But that doesn't explain why the level isn't completed.
Wait, maybe I need to look at the level from a fresh perspective. Let me check the history to see what the initial state was and what actions led to the current state.
Actually, I've spent too many actions on this level. Let me try a completely different approach. Maybe the goal is to click on the yellow square at (3, 30) to do something.
Or maybe the goal is to click on the colored squares in the top bar to change the color of the magenta squares.
Let me try clicking on the pink square in the top bar at (1, 1) to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the pink square in the top bar at (1, 1)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 1, 'col': 1}])
print(f"After click on pink: level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All non-background nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    rmin, cmin = min(rows), min(cols)
    if n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{rmin},{cmin}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
